title: Honey Mustard Pork Chops with Rice
description: These delicious honey mustard pork chops are pan-seared to perfection and served with fluffy rice, making for a quick and satisfying family meal that even picky eaters will love.
instructions: In a medium bowl, whisk together honey, Dijon mustard, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, and pepper to create the honey mustard sauce.
Pat the pork chops dry with paper towels. Season lightly with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Sear pork chops for 3-4 minutes per side, until golden brown.
Pour half of the honey mustard sauce over the pork chops. Reduce heat to medium-low, cover, and simmer for 5-7 minutes, or until pork chops are cooked through (internal temperature of 145°F/63°C).

While pork chops are simmering, combine white rice and chicken broth in a saucepan. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until liquid is absorbed and rice is tender.
Remove pork chops from skillet and let rest for a few minutes. If desired, reduce the remaining sauce in the skillet over medium heat until slightly thickened.
Serve pork chops with rice, drizzled with the remaining honey mustard sauce.
tips: For extra flavor, marinate the pork chops in a small amount of the honey mustard sauce for at least 30 minutes before cooking.
If your kids prefer a milder flavor, use less Dijon mustard and more honey in the sauce.
Add some steamed green beans or broccoli to the plate for a complete and healthy meal.
